Protein names: Homocysteine S-methyltransferase 2 (EC 2.1.1.10) (S-adenosylmethionine metabolism protein 4) (S-methylmethionine:homocysteine methyltransferase 2) (SMM:Hcy S-methyltransferase 2)
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=S-methyl-L-methionine + L-homocysteine = 2 L-methionine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:26337, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57844, ChEBI:CHEBI:58199, ChEBI:CHEBI:58252; EC=2.1.1.10;
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Homocysteine S-methyltransferase involved in the conversion of S-adenosylmethionine (AdoMet) to methionine to control the methionine/AdoMet ratio. Also converts S-methylmethionine (SMM) to methionine. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:11013242}.

Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=S-methyl-L-methionine + L-homocysteine = 2 L-methionine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:26337, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57844, ChEBI:CHEBI:58199, ChEBI:CHEBI:58252; EC=2.1.1.10; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:10747987};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Catalyzes methyl transfer from S-methylmethionine (SMM) to adenosyl-L-homocysteine (AdoMet). SMM degradation (by HMT-1, HMT-2 and HMT-3) and biosynthesis (by MMT1) constitute the SMM cycle in plants, which is probably required to achieve short term control of AdoMet level.
